Two well-known Aussie influencers have slammed the Paris Olympics opening ceremony, claiming one scene shown on TV made a mockery of Jesus.

The Last Supper — the biblical scene showing Jesus and his disciples eating together before Judas betrayed him — was reimagined for Friday night’s ceremony.

In stark contrast to the classic 15th-century interpretation by Leonardo da Vinci, the contemporary depiction featured a woman in a halo crown with her hands in a heart shape, flanked by a number of drag queens and a child.

Business owner Chloe Szepanowski and her partner Mitchell Orval took to Instagram to express their anger at the performance.

“Did anyone else see them mocking Jesus at the Olympic Games?” Orval asked his followers.

“If it made me as angry as I did, let it be a comforting reminder — they wouldn’t be doing this unless he is the truth!”

Szepanowski said the opening ceremony was the worst one she had seen.

“Well this is just the absolute worst one yet,” She said.

“This year they are mocking God. This is appalling but all the more reason to follow Jesus.”

Their social media posts came just after France’s clergy expressed outrage at the “mockery of Christianity”.

“The opening ceremony proposed by the Catholic Organisation for Faith and Sport offered the whole world wonderful moments of beauty and joy. It was rich in emotion and it was universally acclaimed,” A statement from France’s Conference of Bishops said.

“The ceremony unfortunately included scenes of derision and mockery of Christianity, which we deplore very deeply.”

- With DailyMail

‘SHOW SOME RESPECT’

title: Aussie influencers Chloe Szepanowski and Mitchell Orval slam Olympic Opening Ceremony for ‘mocking Jesus’ | PerthNow
description: Two well-known Aussie influencers have slammed the Paris Olympics opening ceremony, claiming one scene shown on TV made a mockery of God.
